sections.paragraph:
ἀνασκάπτω pf. pass. ptc. n. pl. ἀνεσκαμμένα (Aristot., Theophr. et al.; Ps. 7:16; 79:17; Joseph.) dig up, raze, destroy Ac 15:16 v.l. (s. κατασκάπτω).

  1. pf. = perfect

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

ptc. ptc. = participle

  1. pl. = plural

Aristot Aristot , various works, IV b.c.—List 5

Theophr Theophr , IV–III b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

Ps. Ps. = pseudo, pseudonymous

Joseph Joseph This abbr. used when no specific texts are cited (s. Jos.); I a.d.—List 5

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 71
